ingredients.item:
DOLMA
8 small, oval-shaped yellow onions (see Notes)
2/3 pound (300 g) lean ground beef
ingredients.original:
DOLMA
8 small, oval-shaped yellow onions (see Notes)
2/3 pound (300 g) lean ground beef

ingredients.item: 1½ cups (300 g) short-grain white rice, rinsed until the water runs clear and drained completely
ingredients.original: 1½ cups (300 g) short-grain white rice, rinsed until the water runs clear and drained completely

ingredients.item: ½ cup (25 g) finely chopped fresh parsley
ingredients.original: ½ cup (25 g) finely chopped fresh parsley

ingredients.item:
BAKING LIQUID
½ cup (120 g) tomato paste
3 tablespoons pomegranate molasses
ingredients.original:
BAKING LIQUID
½ cup (120 g) tomato paste
3 tablespoons pomegranate molasses

ingredients.item: ¼ cup (60 ml) fresh lemon juice
ingredients.original: ¼ cup (60 ml) fresh lemon juice

ingredients.item: 1 Preheat the oven to 450°F (230°C) and position a rack on the bottom level.
ingredients.original: 1 Preheat the oven to 450°F (230°C) and position a rack on the bottom level.

ingredients.item: 2 TO MAKE THE DOLMA: Peel an onion without slicing off the ends, then, using a paring knife, dig around the end where the root is and carve it out slightly, about ¼ inch (6 mm) deep (this helps separate the layers). After doing this, discard the root and separate all the layers slightly at the end to expose them. Make a long cut along the length of the onion through all the layers to the center; do not cut past the center. Repeat with the remaining onions. Place the onions in a medium microwave-safe glass bowl and microwave for 1 minute to soften the layers slightly (you can also boil them on the stovetop for 1 minute). Let cool while you prepare the rest of the ingredients.
ingredients.original: 2 TO MAKE THE DOLMA: Peel an onion without slicing off the ends, then, using a paring knife, dig around the end where the root is and carve it out slightly, about ¼ inch (6 mm) deep (this helps separate the layers). After doing this, discard the root and separate all the layers slightly at the end to expose them. Make a long cut along the length of the onion through all the layers to the center; do not cut past the center. Repeat with the remaining onions. Place the onions in a medium microwave-safe glass bowl and microwave for 1 minute to soften the layers slightly (you can also boil them on the stovetop for 1 minute). Let cool while you prepare the rest of the ingredients.

ingredients.item: 3 In a large bowl, combine the ground beef, garlic, chopped onion, red pepper, rice, parsley, 2 teaspoons salt, black pepper, cumin, allspice, dried dill, dried mint, pomegranate molasses, 3 tablespoons tomato paste, and 2 tablespoons lemon juice and mix well.
ingredients.original: 3 In a large bowl, combine the ground beef, garlic, chopped onion, red pepper, rice, parsley, 2 teaspoons salt, black pepper, cumin, allspice, dried dill, dried mint, pomegranate molasses, 3 tablespoons tomato paste, and 2 tablespoons lemon juice and mix well.

ingredients.item: 4 To stuff the onions, use your fingers to carefully dislodge every layer of each onion and pop them out, one by one. Gently pry the layers apart from both sides until you reach the center. Repeat with the remaining onions until you have them all in single layers. Place roughly 2 tablespoons of the stuffing inside each onion layer, then roll it closed. The ends of the onion should fold over one another by at least ½ inch (13 mm); if you cannot do this, it means you have stuffed it too much. Place the stuffed onions in a 9 x 13-inch (23 x 33 cm) baking dish, packed tightly side by side with the seam sides down, to help them stay closed; they should be snuggly packed in.
ingredients.original: 4 To stuff the onions, use your fingers to carefully dislodge every layer of each onion and pop them out, one by one. Gently pry the layers apart from both sides until you reach the center. Repeat with the remaining onions until you have them all in single layers. Place roughly 2 tablespoons of the stuffing inside each onion layer, then roll it closed. The ends of the onion should fold over one another by at least ½ inch (13 mm); if you cannot do this, it means you have stuffed it too much. Place the stuffed onions in a 9 x 13-inch (23 x 33 cm) baking dish, packed tightly side by side with the seam sides down, to help them stay closed; they should be snuggly packed in.
